advantages: Clear objective: minimize error against known labels, making problem formulation and success criteria straightforward.
Strong performance on well-defined prediction tasks when sufficient quality labels exist (classification, regression, ranking).
Mature tooling — scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow — with standard fit/predict APIs and established evaluation metrics.
Interpretable workflow: collect (x, y), train, validate on held-out labels, deploy predict on new x.
Directly maps to business outcomes when labels reflect the desired prediction (churn, fraud, price, diagnosis).
disadvantages: Requires labeled data, which can be expensive, slow, or biased to obtain (expert annotation, privacy constraints).
Model quality is capped by label noise and coverage — wrong or sparse labels teach incorrect mappings.
Poor generalization when training distribution differs from deployment (covariate shift, new categories).
Not suited when the goal is exploratory structure discovery without predefined targets — unsupervised methods may fit better.
Risk of overfitting labels on small datasets without proper validation splits and regularization.
